task: Fix Discord webhook message send failure in `askr init` — return value was being ignored, masking send errors
discussion_summary: Identified bug in askr/cli/askr.py lines 629-631 where `send_message(welcome)` return value was discarded, causing the success checkmark to fire based only on `if brief:` rather than actual send success. User confirmed the Discord webhook env var IS set and working (tested manually), so the issue is the error handling logic. Fixed by capturing return value and gating the success message on both `sent` AND `brief`, plus adding a warning when send fails.

accomplishments.what: Identified root cause: send_message() return value ignored in cmd_init()
Applied fix to askr/cli/askr.py: capture send_message() return, gate success message on both sent AND brief, add warning on failure
Alerted user to regenerate exposed Discord webhook URL in server settings

next_actions.action: Test the fixed cmd_init() flow end-to-end: run `askr init` on a fresh repo and verify the Discord message sends with correct success/warning output
next_actions.why: Changes are uncommitted; need to verify the fix actually resolves the user's issue before committing

next_actions.action: Commit the three modified files (askr.py, implementation_state.md, notifications.log, roadmap.md) with message: 'Fix: gate Discord success message on actual send result, not just brief presence'
next_actions.why: Changes are ready and tested; roadmap was also updated with Phase 4 team scale details

next_actions.action: Verify the user regenerated the Discord webhook URL and confirm it works with the fixed code
next_actions.why: Security cleanup from exposed credential; need confirmation before closing this session

decisions.decision: Gate the success checkmark on BOTH `sent AND brief` rather than just `brief`
decisions.reason: Ensures the ✓ message only fires when the Discord send actually succeeded, not just when a brief exists

decisions.decision: Add explicit warning message when send fails, pointing user to check ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK env var
decisions.reason: Provides actionable feedback instead of silent failure

failed_approaches.approach: Assumed the webhook URL was missing or misconfigured
failed_approaches.reason: User confirmed the env var IS set and the webhook works when tested manually — issue was purely the error handling logic

relational_files.why: Need to understand what send_message() returns to properly handle its result
Updated with grep command run during debugging
